expectations.text: The response proposes at least 3 separate tickets instead of one mega-ticket.
expectations.evidence: Proposed 6 tickets (Design tokens, Docs, Shared theme manager, Android wiring, iOS wiring, QA pass), well above the minimum of 3.

expectations.text: The response groups tickets by area (e.g., UI, backend, design, platform-specific).
expectations.evidence: Tickets are grouped by area: design (Design tokens), documentation (Docs), shared infrastructure (Shared theme manager), platform-specific (Android wiring, iOS wiring), and testing (QA pass).

expectations.text: The response calls out dependencies and sequencing (which tickets block others).
expectations.evidence: Conversation confirms a sequencing diagram was included, showing dependency ordering between tickets.

expectations.text: Each proposed ticket has a clear, outcome-oriented title that describes what it accomplishes.
expectations.evidence: Tickets have outcome-oriented titles (Design tokens, Docs, Shared theme manager, Android wiring, iOS wiring, QA pass) and each includes scope boundaries and acceptance criteria with GIVEN/WHEN/THEN format.

expectations.text: The response surfaces hidden scope such as empty states, loading states, error states, or backwards compatibility concerns.
expectations.evidence: Surfaces edge cases including first launch behavior, system theme detection, and accessibility contrast concerns.

eval_feedback.overall: All 5 expectations passed. The old_skill baseline produced a thorough breakdown with 6 tickets, clear grouping by area, sequencing, outcome-oriented titles with acceptance criteria, and surfaced relevant edge cases.
